meta.setting: Jefferson State frontier, opening around French Gulch, Clear Creek, Shasta, and the Klamath mountains
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western: horseback travel, camp life, hunting, debt, law pressure, outlaw choice, and a hard 1899 frontier tone

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Courthouse, commerce, iron, brick, and northern California record-keeping material.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear
research_grounding.use: Black bear food behavior and sign for animal-reference objects.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Mountain Lion
research_grounding.use: Mountain lion sign and livestock conflict for track-reference objects.

image_prompt.subject: a cluttered sheriff-clerk desk used as a frontier reference shelf: folded trail maps, cartridge box, coffee tin, animal track sketches, claim papers, and a rain-warped county ledger
image_prompt.scene: interior office beside a smoky stove, late night; boots drying under a chair, pine rain ticking the window, no readable labels or page text
image_prompt.composition: square top-down three-quarter still life with every item readable as game reference but no text legible; ledger anchors the center, track sketches and map corners circle it like working tools
image_prompt.camera: fixed game inventory-closeup angle with mild lens distortion, baked shadows, and practical object spacing
material_cues: creased paper softened by damp air
coffee grounds around a tin lid
soft pine desktop cut with pocketknife marks
charcoal animal-track sketches blurred enough to avoid readable labels
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the desk feel used by a practical person who can find a receipt in the dark but never throws anything away. It should be reference material with dirt under its nails.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine inventory/still-life render
render_traits: modest texture resolution on paper, brass, leather, and pine
hard-edged ambient occlusion under overlapping objects
slight low-poly geometry on cartridges, cups, and folded paper
warm lamp light with cool window rain
no clean UI overlay or modern graphic layout
style_modifiers.palette: lamp amber, pine brown, ink blue-black, dull brass, stove-charcoal, wet paper gray, coffee umber, and cold window blue.

text_rendering.instructions: Avoid readable words, numbers, labels, or map text. Marks may appear as period-appropriate blurred strokes only.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The desk, ledger, maps, track sketches, and camp objects are fully readable as shapes.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 frontier work.
There is no readable text, logo, UI, or modern office object.
The image feels like a practical reference shelf, not a decorative treasure spread.
